user_query:
I’m prepping my portfolio templates for class and need a quick automated check of external assets. Please write and run a command-line script that scans all HTML files under input/templates/, extracts every external CSS/JS/font URL referenced by <link rel="stylesheet" ...> and <script src="..."> tags, attempts to download each unique URL, and builds a concise audit. Requirements:

Inputs:
- Scan every .html file under input/templates/ (recursive).

What to extract:
- Only external (http/https) URLs from <link rel="stylesheet" href="..."> and <script src="...">.
- Deduplicate URLs, but keep track of every template file that referenced each URL.

Network/download behavior:
- Use HTTP GET with a 10-second timeout and follow redirects.
- If the response is 200 and content-size is <= 3 MiB, save the body under output/downloads/<host>/<sanitized-path> (create directories as needed).
- If larger than 3 MiB, don’t save the body; record it as skipped_large.
- For non-200 responses or network errors, record as fail without saving a body.

Outputs (create these paths):
- output/report/assets_audit.json: an array of objects with fields: original_url, host, referenced_by (array of file paths found), first_seen_line (1-based), status (ok|fail|skipped_large), http_status (number or null), final_url (after redirects if any), content_type (or null), bytes (or null), sha256 (hex of saved body or null).
- output/report/assets_audit.csv: same rows/columns as the JSON (flat columns; referenced_by may be a semicolon-delimited list).
- output/report/summary.txt: totals in plain text with one "key: value" per line for total_urls_found (counting duplicates across files), unique_urls, ok_count, fail_count, skipped_large_count.
- output/downloads/...: saved files for ok items as described.
- output/logs/run.log: a timestamped run log with at least start time, end time, number of HTML files scanned, and unique URL count.

Constraints:
- Do not hardcode any URLs; discover them only by reading the local HTML files.
- Treat relative/local asset paths as out of scope; only attempt remote http/https URLs.
- Preserve one record per unique URL while aggregating all referencing files/lines.

Invocation:
- Provide a runnable script at scripts/audit_assets (you may choose a common extension like .py or .js) that accepts two args: input_dir and output_dir.
- Add a one-line run command in run_audit.sh that executes your script against input/templates and output.
- Run the script so the specified outputs are generated in the workspace.

Deliverables:
- scripts/audit_assets.*
- run_audit.sh
- output/report/assets_audit.json
- output/report/assets_audit.csv
- output/report/summary.txt
- output/logs/run.log
- output/downloads/ (if any ok items) 

I’ll use this to spot broken CDN links in my templates before I submit them for review, so please keep the output fields exact and the paths as specified. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
